lts.lt_definition: I can describe who I am including my name, appearance, and personal characteristics.
I can identify and express what I like about myself and what matters to me.
I can describe what happens in my body when I experience different emotions.
I can recognize and name emotions that external stimuli evoke in me.
I can use a variety of specific words to describe my emotions in different situations.
I can explain what helps me feel better when I am upset or frustrated.
The student practises calming strategies like controlled breathing when experiencing strong emotions.
I can identify what I share in common with others and how I am different from them.
I can participate in creating environments where everyone feels they belong.
I can identify and communicate positive qualities about my classmates and describe actions that build good friendships.
The student enacts a generous orientation toward sharing resources and opportunities with others.
I can identify basic emotions from facial expressions and body language.
I can explain how someone might feel in different situations.
I can identify and articulate specific goals I want to achieve.
The student enacts leadership by initiating activities and inviting others to participate.
The student sustains openness and confidence when encountering unfamiliar situations.
I can identify the difference between behaviors that hurt others and behaviors that help others feel good.
The student actively seeks opportunities to make others feel valued and supported.
I can clearly, firmly and politely communicate my needs and wants in conflict situations.
I can recognize unfair situations and stand up for myself and others appropriately.
I can distinguish between accidental and deliberate hurts and identify internal versus external factors contributing to harm.
I can participate in processes that address harm and work toward healing relationships.
I can explain how lies can escalate and provide reasons why truth-telling is beneficial.
I can identify the responsibilities that correspond to specific rights and freedoms.
I can explain how natural processes and human actions work together to support growth and development.
I can compare experiences that bring short-term satisfaction with those that create enduring fulfillment.
I can identify different roles I play and explain how my behavior changes across these contexts.
I can describe my current strengths and qualities and explain what capabilities I might need to develop.
I can explain what matters most to me and justify why these things are important.
I can explain emotions as a continuum and discuss when emotions control us versus when we control them.
I can discuss how music, dance, and drama influence emotional states.
I can identify emotional vocabulary and explain how thinking strategies affect feelings.
I can evaluate different anger management strategies and justify which option is most appropriate.
I can write about ways in which everyone is unique and explain why this uniqueness is valuable.
I can explain what The Golden Rule is, how it appears across major religions, and what it means in practice.
The student holds a sustained orientation toward balanced participation in conversations through appropriate talking and listening.
The student enacts regular small acts of kindness and goes out of their way to help others.
The student practises effective collaboration within groups to achieve shared targets.
I can explain what emotions someone might experience in different life situations.
I can identify emotions by interpreting voice tone, body language, and facial expressions.
I can explain what it means to have a personal best and how improvement happens over time.
The student holds a consistent orientation toward self-improvement and learning from mistakes.
The student enacts awareness of exclusion and consistently includes those who have been left out.
I can identify specific things to be grateful for and explain why they matter to me.

lt_names: Identifying Sources of Gratitude and Anticipation

lts.lt_definition: I can identify situations where positive thinking helped and explain the difference it made.

lt_names: Identifying Sources of Gratitude and Anticipation

lts.lt_definition: I can identify when a situation is not safe and determine when to withdraw.
The student stands up for others who are less able to advocate for themselves.
I can identify how much responsibility belongs to me, others, and chance when harm occurs.
The student offers to make up for harm caused across various situations.
I can discuss why people don't always get what they deserve and explain what this means for how we see the world and others.
I can choose inclusion and equity principles and decide how to put them into practice in specific contexts.
I can choose a symbol that represents what matters most in life and explain my reasoning.
I can identify someone who exemplifies living well and analyze their meaningful actions.

lts.lt_name: Articulating Personal Values and Character Development
lts.lt_definition: I can explain my core values, character strengths, and how these guide my life choices.

lt_names: Articulating Personal Values and Character Development
Articulating Personal Values and Character Strengths
Articulating Personal Values and Character Strengths

lts.lt_definition: The student practises ongoing reflection on their developing identity and personal growth.
prerequisite_lts: Articulating Personal Values and Character Development

lt_names: Articulating Personal Values and Character Development
Articulating Personal Values and Character Strengths
Articulating Personal Values and Character Strengths

lts.lt_definition: I can explain how the amygdala and mirroring neurons affect emotional responses and provide examples of their operation.
I can analyze the emotional content of characters and experiences in films and explain their impact on my own emotional responses.
I can identify multiple emotion regulation strategies and evaluate their advantages and disadvantages.
I can reflect on emotional experiences during learning and describe the range of emotions encountered.
I can analyze how friendships support or undermine resilience development.
I can explain why shared humanity matters for creating a just and equitable world.
I can collaborate to design effective materials that challenge discrimination and promote inclusion.
I can demonstrate active listening through verbal and non-verbal attention, appropriate responses, and clarifying questions.
I can explain what respect means, how it is demonstrated, and why it matters in relationships.
I can initiate and maintain meaningful conversations with unfamiliar people.
I can identify appropriate timing for social interactions based on contextual factors and others' states.
The student holds a default orientation of curiosity about others' experiences rather than making quick judgments.
I can break down complex tasks into 8-12 achievable sequential steps.
I can evaluate potential risks against expected outcomes when making decisions.
The student actively helps others achieve their goals and perform well.
I can reframe problems as solution-oriented goals that describe desired outcomes rather than what to eliminate.
The student sustains a consistent practice of identifying and recording daily blessings across multiple life domains.
I can identify the short- and long-term outcomes of overt aggression, passive aggression, timidity and appropriate assertiveness.
The student checks on intention before jumping to a negative conclusion in potential conflicts.
I can list ways to say how something could have been done differently.
The student responds positively to attempts to repair harm caused.
I can identify and explain factors that influence voting decisions in democratic elections.
The student enacts a sustained commitment to environmental protection through active participation in conservation projects.
I can engage thoughtfully in discussions about what constitutes a meaningful or good life.
I can identify and articulate what gives my own life significance and purpose.
I can identify differences between various emotional states and recognize when to seek support.

lt_names: Distinguishing Emotional States and Support Systems
Distinguishing Emotional States and Support Systems

lts.lt_definition: I can explain how emotions were manipulated in historical events and analyze the methods used.

lt_names: Distinguishing Emotional States and Support Systems
Distinguishing Emotional States and Support Systems

lts.lt_definition: I can engage thoughtfully in discussions about how people feel regarding controversial issues or emotional situations.
I can evaluate the usefulness of mindfulness and meditation as resilience strategies.
The student practises using physical, social, and cognitive strategies regularly to maintain emotional resilience.
I can identify instances of dehumanization, analyze their contributing factors, and explain their consequences.
I can identify and propose ways to practice shared humanity across individual, community, and social levels.
I can explain what trust means, how it is demonstrated, and why it is important for healthy relationships.
I can work effectively with a group to complete projects and analyze factors that promote successful collaboration.
I can assess the emotional content of situations and explain what actions would improve or worsen them.
I can represent different viewpoints of the same events through multiple character perspectives.
I can reflect on a personal achievement and analyze what contributed to success.
I can identify plans for the future with fall-back plans if these don't happen.
I can identify what helps resist peer pressure to act according to my own values.
I can interpret the same event from multiple perspectives and explain how different viewpoints change the meaning.
I can articulate hopeful visions for the future and identify specific actions to work toward those outcomes.
I can analyze a conflict scenario by identifying what each side wants and generating multiple possible resolutions with their trade-offs.
I can facilitate negotiation between conflicting parties to reach a workable compromise that addresses core interests.
I can evaluate the meaning and importance of altruism as a guiding principle for social action.
I can research social justice issues and create actionable plans to address them.
I can engage in reasoned discussion about ethical values and their application to real-world situations.

lt_names: Researching and Planning Social Justice Solutions

lts.lt_definition: I can research social justice challenges and develop actionable plans to address them.

lt_names: Researching and Planning Social Justice Solutions

lts.lt_definition: I can write or speak about the principles or values that will guide my decisions.
I can write a personal statement setting out my aspirations for being, becoming and contributing.
